name: helm-chart-scaffolding
description: Design, organize, and manage Helm charts for templating and packaging Kubernetes applications with reusable configurations. Use when creating Helm charts, packaging Kubernetes applications, or implementing templated deployments.
capabilities: Create new Helm charts with proper structure and templates
Generate Chart.yaml and values.yaml with industry best practices
Implement templating for reusable Kubernetes manifests
Validate Helm charts using the included validation script
Set up multi-environment deployments with values files
Follow Helm chart conventions and directory structure
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 187 static findings are false positives. The skill provides Helm chart scaffolding templates, documentation, and a validation script. The scanner incorrectly flagged placeholder text, documentation code examples, and standard shell scripting patterns as security issues.
valueStatement: This skill helps DevOps engineers scaffold Helm charts from scratch with best practices for packaging and deploying applications to Kubernetes clusters.
limitations: Does not deploy charts to clusters - only creates chart structure
Does not manage Helm repositories or chart publishing
Does not provide runtime secrets management integration
Requires Helm CLI installed for validation features

useCases.title: New Kubernetes Application Setup
useCases.description: Quickly scaffold a production-ready Helm chart for a new microservice with security defaults, resource limits, and health probes pre-configured.

useCases.title: Multi-Environment Configuration
useCases.description: Create structured values files for development, staging, and production environments with appropriate resource allocations and configuration overrides.

useCases.title: Chart Validation and Best Practices
useCases.description: Use the built-in validation script to check charts for common issues, security misconfigurations, and Helm best practices compliance.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Chart Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new Helm chart for my application called 'myapp' with version 1.0.0. Include a Deployment, Service, and ConfigMap. Use the helm-chart-scaffolding skill.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to quickly generate a complete Helm chart structure

promptTemplates.title: Production Chart with Security
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a production-ready Helm chart with security best practices including pod security context, resource limits, liveness and readiness probes, and network policies. Use the helm-chart-scaffolding skill.
promptTemplates.scenario: User needs a production-grade chart with security hardening

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Environment Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Helm chart with separate values files for dev, staging, and production environments. Include appropriate resource allocations and replica counts for each environment. Use the helm-chart-scaffolding skill.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to manage multiple environments with the same chart

promptTemplates.title: Validate Existing Chart
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the validate-chart.sh script to validate my existing Helm chart at ./mychart. Check for best practices, security settings, and common issues. Use the helm-chart-scaffolding skill.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to validate an existing chart for issues

bestPractices: Always define resource requests and limits in values.yaml to prevent resource starvation
Use liveness and readiness probes for proper container health monitoring
Separate configuration from code using values files for each environment
Include Chart.yaml and values.yaml validation in CI/CD pipelines
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode secrets in values.yaml - use external secret operators
Avoid using latest tag for container images - specify exact versions
Do not skip validation - always test charts with helm lint and template
Avoid putting sensitive data in ConfigMaps - use Secrets or external secret management

faq.question: What is Helm chart scaffolding?
faq.answer: Helm chart scaffolding is the process of creating a standardized directory structure and template files for packaging Kubernetes applications. It includes Chart.yaml, values.yaml, and templates for common Kubernetes resources.

faq.question: Do I need Helm installed to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need Helm installed to use the validation script features. The chart creation and template generation work without Helm, but validation requires the Helm CLI.

faq.question: Can this skill deploy my application to Kubernetes?
faq.answer: No, this skill only creates and validates Helm chart structure. To deploy, use 'helm install' or 'helm upgrade' commands with the generated chart.

faq.question: How do I manage secrets with Helm charts?
faq.answer: This skill creates the chart structure but does not include secrets. For production, use external secret operators like External Secrets Operator or HashiCorp Vault integration.

faq.question: What Kubernetes resources are included in the templates?
faq.answer: The templates include Deployment, Service, ConfigMap, Secret, Ingress, ServiceAccount, and PodDisruptionBudget. Additional resources can be added to the templates directory.

faq.question: How do I validate my chart after creation?
faq.answer: Run the validate-chart.sh script from the scripts directory: ./scripts/validate-chart.sh /path/to/chart. It checks structure, linting, template rendering, and security best practices.
